question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST arbitrary protobuf bytes to /api/v1/network/fees with no authentication required, triggering fee calculation on a crafted TokenFeeScheduleUpdate transaction body, causing the fee estimator to return a fee value outside the design parameters defined by the on-chain fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a highVolumeThrottle parameter value of exactly 10000 (the annotated @Max boundary) to POST /api/v1/network/fees, causing FeeEstimationFeeContext.getHighVolumeThrottleUtilization() to return 10000 and the fee multiplier to be computed at maximum congestion, producing a fee estimate that is orders of magnitude above the actual network fee?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user set mode=STATE and highVolumeThrottle=0 in POST /api/v1/network/fees while simultaneously setting mode=INTRINSIC in a parallel request, exploiting the difference in FeeEstimationFeeContext construction paths (withFeeContext vs plain TransactionFeeContext) to obtain two divergent fee estimates for the same transaction, allowing the user to cherry-pick the lower estimate and underpay fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: refreshStateCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user flood POST /api/v1/network/fees with high-volume requests during the PT10M window between scheduled refreshStateCalculator() calls, causing the fee calculator to operate on a stale fee schedule and return fee estimates that do not reflect the latest on-chain fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TransactionFeeContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a protobuf Transaction with an extremely large sigMap containing thousands of SignaturePair entries, causing numTxnSignatures() to return an inflated count, which drives the fee calculator to produce an artificially high fee estimate that misrepresents the true cost of the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: numTxnBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with padded or redundant protobuf fields to inflate the value returned by Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord(), causing the byte-based fee component to be computed above the design maximum for that transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TransactionFeeContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction that contains both non-empty signedTransactionBytes and non-empty bodyBytes, exploiting the if/else branch ordering in TransactionFeeContext to have the signedTransactionBytes path parse a different transaction body than what is in bodyBytes, causing the fee to be calculated for a different transaction type than the one actually described?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a protobuf Transaction whose bodyBytes encodes a TOKENCREATION transaction body with a maximum number of custom fees, causing FeeTokenStore.getCustomFees() to return a large list and the STATE-mode fee calculator to compute a fee significantly higher than the INTRINSIC-mode estimate, creating a discrepancy that can be exploited to misrepresent transaction costs?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCustomFees() in FeeTokenStore] Can an unprivileged external user reference a token ID in a fee estimation request that has a large number of custom fees persisted in the custom_fee table, causing FeeTokenStore.getCustomFees() to return a count-inflated list of CustomFee.DEFAULT placeholders, driving the STATE-mode fee estimate above the design-intended ceiling for that token type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCustomFees() in FeeTopicStore] Can an unprivileged external user reference a topic ID with many fixed fees in a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE fee estimation request, causing FeeTopicStore.getCustomFees() to return an inflated list of FixedCustomFee.DEFAULT placeholders, resulting in a STATE-mode fee estimate that exceeds the design parameters for topic message submission?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with an empty body (neither signedTransactionBytes nor bodyBytes set), bypassing the IllegalArgumentException guard and reaching calculateTxFee() with a null or default TransactionBody, causing the fee calculator to return a zero or undefined fee that is outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: functionality()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TransactionBody that encodes an unknown or reserved HederaFunctionality type, causing functionality() to throw UnknownHederaFunctionality wrapped in IllegalStateException, which is then re-thrown uncaught from estimateFees(), leaking internal stack trace information that reveals fee schedule structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: refreshStateCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the @Async initFeeSchedule() call at ApplicationReadyEvent and the @Scheduled refreshStateCalculator() call, causing feeManager.updateSimpleFees() to be called concurrently with getSimpleFeeCalculator(), resulting in a fee calculator that operates on a partially-updated fee schedule and returns fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONTRACTCALL transaction body to POST /api/v1/network/fees and receive a fee estimate of 0 (as documented in test expectations), exploiting the fact that ContractCall fees are paid in gas and the hbar fee is cleared, to misrepresent the true cost of a contract call transaction to downstream consumers of the API?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: activeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FeeEstimationFeeContext.activeRate() always returns null, causing any fee calculation path that depends on the active exchange rate to use a fallback or zero rate, producing fee estimates in tinycents that do not correctly reflect the HBAR/USD exchange rate and are therefore outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getGasPriceInTinycents()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FeeEstimationFeeContext.getGasPriceInTinycents() always returns 0, causing gas-priced transactions submitted to the STATE-mode fee estimator to receive a zero gas price component, producing a fee estimate that is below the design-intended minimum for gas-consuming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with signedTransactionBytes that wraps a TransactionBody with a different transaction type than what is declared in the outer Transaction wrapper, causing the fee to be calculated for the inner type while the outer type is used for routing, resulting in a fee estimate that does not match the actual transaction being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: numTxnSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with zero signatures (empty sigMap) to obtain a fee estimate that does not account for signature verification costs, then submit the actual transaction with many signatures, causing the network to charge more than the estimated fee and violating the design invariant that estimates should bound actual costs?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call POST /api/v1/network/fees with mode=STATE and varying highVolumeThrottle values (0 through 10000) to enumerate the fee multiplier curve, extracting the congestion pricing model from the FeeEstimationFeeContext.getHighVolumeThrottleUtilization() implementation and using this information to time transactions for minimum fee payment?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toResponse() in NetworkController] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes calculateTxFee() to return a FeeResult with a negative totalTinycents value due to integer overflow in the fee components, and have this negative fee estimate returned as a valid response from POST /api/v1/network/fees, misleading clients into believing a transaction has negative cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE transaction body to the fee estimator with a token ID that has no fee_schedule_key set, causing the fee calculator to compute a fee for an operation that would be rejected on-chain, producing a misleading fee estimate that is outside the design parameters for valid transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: refreshStateCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lastFeeScheduleTimestamp AtomicLong comparison (latestTimestamp > lastFeeScheduleTimestamp.get()) by observing that if the database contains a fee schedule with a timestamp equal to the current lastFeeScheduleTimestamp, the refresh is skipped, causing the fee calculator to permanently use a stale schedule if the DB timestamp never advances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction with a CRYPTOTRANSFER body that includes an extremely large number of token transfer entries, causing numTxnBytes() to return a value that drives the byte-based fee component above the design maximum, producing a fee estimate that exceeds the on-chain limit for CryptoTransfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a Transaction body with a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE containing a 2000-byte message (LONG_MESSAGE_BYTES as seen in tests) to obtain a fee estimate, then compare it against the estimate for a 1-byte message, and use the difference to infer the per-byte fee rate from the fee schedule, extracting confidential pricing parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: FeeEstimationState constructor] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded exchange rate in FeeEstimationState (centEquiv=12, hbarEquiv=1 with expiration Long.MAX_VALUE) by submitting fee estimation requests when the actual network exchange rate differs significantly, causing the fee estimate to be computed using a stale rate that is outside the design parameters for accurate fee prediction?
